headline: How TRYPS Works: Plan a Group Trip From Invite to Settle-Up
alternativeHeadline: A Step-by-Step Guide to Using TRYPS for Friend Group Travel
description: Step-by-step guide to using TRYPS: invite friends via iMessage, set the group vibe, vote on activities, plan day-by-day, and split expenses — all in one app.

image.caption: TRYPS activities tab showing the voting block for a New York group trip

name: TRYPS
description: Group trip planning app for friends — invite via iMessage, vote on activities, share an itinerary, and split costs in one place.

name: How TRYPS Works: From Invite to Settle-Up
How to use TRYPS to plan a group trip
description: A 5-step guide to planning a group trip with friends using TRYPS — from the iMessage invite to the final expense split.

step.name: Create the trip and text your group
step.text: Open TRYPS, set your destination and dates, add a cover photo, then tap Text Friends to share the trip via iMessage. Friends join via the link without downloading anything.

step.name: Set your group's vibe
step.text: Each member answers six quick prompts: pace, budget style, food priority, group dynamic, activity type, and planning depth. TRYPS uses the overlap to suggest activities that match the whole group.

step.name: Vote on activities together
step.text: Add ideas to the Voting Block. Anyone can thumbs-up or thumbs-down. Drag activities to Confirmed when the group is locked in. Browse Discover for vibe-matched popular spots.

step.name: See the day-by-day itinerary
step.text: Confirmed activities flow into the Itinerary tab automatically. Each day shows weather, a 24-hour timeline, and slots for activities. Tap any day to add or reorder.

step.name: Track expenses and settle up
step.text: Add expenses with who paid and how to split. TRYPS keeps a running balance and shows All Settled when nobody owes anyone. Settle in one tap when the trip ends.

mainEntity.name: Do my friends need to download TRYPS to join my trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. When you tap Text Friends, TRYPS sends an iMessage with a join link. Friends tap the link and land directly inside the trip — they can vote on activities, see the itinerary, and add expenses without installing the app first.
Yes. TRYPS is free for the host and every guest. There is no paywall on creating a trip, inviting friends, voting on activities, building an itinerary, or splitting expenses.

mainEntity.name: How does group voting on activities work in TRYPS?

acceptedAnswer.text: Anyone in the trip can add an activity to the Voting Block. Each member casts a thumbs-up or thumbs-down. Vote counts are visible to the whole group, so it's clear when something has consensus. Drag a popular activity to Confirmed and it slots into the day-by-day itinerary.

mainEntity.name: How does TRYPS split expenses between friends?

acceptedAnswer.text: When someone pays for something, they add the expense, choose who paid and how it splits — evenly across the group or only across the people involved. TRYPS keeps a running balance and shows All Settled when nobody owes anyone money.

mainEntity.name: Can I plan multiple trips at the same time?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Each trip is a separate space with its own people, activities, itinerary, and expense ledger. Switch between them from the home screen without anything overlapping.

mainEntity.name: What kind of trips work best with TRYPS?

acceptedAnswer.text: Anything that involves more than two people making decisions together — weekend getaways, bachelor and bachelorette trips, ski trips, group beach vacations, multi-city adventures. The bigger the group, the more friction TRYPS removes.

mainEntity.name: What's the best app for planning a group trip with friends?

acceptedAnswer.text: TRYPS combines iMessage invites, group voting, a shared itinerary with weather and timing, and built-in expense splitting in one place. That's the difference: friend groups stop bouncing between WhatsApp, Google Sheets, and Splitwise and coordinate the whole trip from a single app.
